---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# load libraries 
library(dplyr)
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
library(tidyr)
```

```{r}
# load samples
load('data/samples.Rdata')
# load census tables 
load('data/poptable.Rdata')
```

```{r}
# import functions
source('helper_rev.R')
```


```{r}
# define fixed objects
coalitions <- c('CSX', 'CDX', 'Az', 'M5S', 'altri')
# define available adjustment columns
COLS <-  c('gen', 'age', 'emp', 'edu', 'zone', 'pastvote')
# define census tables to iterate over
census_list <- c('edu', 'emp', 'pastvote', 'approx')

# build iteration grid
iter_grid <- expand.grid(
  df_name = names(main),
  census_name = census_list,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# run one sample-census combination
run_one <- function(df_name, census_name, iter_id = NA, n_iter = NA) {
  # print progress
  print(paste('processing', iter_id, 'of', n_iter, df_name, census_name))
  # select census 
  ref_table <- poptable[[census_name]]
  # select data 
  to_adj <- main[[df_name]]
  # check or pastvote 
  census_cols <- colnames(ref_table)[colnames(ref_table) %in% COLS]
  # check available sample columns
  data_cols <- colnames(to_adj)[colnames(to_adj) %in% COLS]
  
  # skip sample-census combinations that cannot be adjusted
  if (!all(census_cols %in% data_cols)) {
    # print skipped combination
    print(paste('skipping', df_name, census_name, '- missing columns in sample'))
    # return empty result
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # unweighted
  unweighted_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'unadjusted',
    uniweighted(to_adj)
  )

  # post-stratification or calibration (the same here)
  poststrat_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'post-stratification',
    poststrat(to_adj, ref_table)
  )

  # multinomial linear model-assisted post-stratification
  multinomial_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'MN post-stratification',
    multinomial(
      to_adj,
      ref_table,
      B = 5 # 1000
    )
  )

  # generate approximate reference sample
  reference_survey <- generate_reference(census = ref_table)
  # logit IPW
  ipw_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'Logit-IPW',
    ps_ipw(to_adj, reference_survey, trim = 5, B = 5)
  )

  # # generate approximate reference sample
  # reference_survey <- generate_reference(census = ref_table)
  # run IPW with a gradient-boosted selection model
  xgboost_out <- nonprob_ipw_xgb(
    # non-probability survey
    data = to_adj,
    # generated reference sample
    reference_survey = reference_survey,
    # bootstrap SEs
    var_method = "bootstrap",
    # bootstrap repetitions
    num_boot = 10 # 1000
  )
  # XGBoost IPW
  xgboost_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'XGboost-IPW',
    xgboost_out
  )

  # DRP
  drp_out <- data.frame(
    sample = df_name,
    census = census_name,
    method = 'DRP',
    nonprob_drp(to_adj, ref_table)
  )

  # # force rstan to avoid parallel chains
  # options(mc.cores = 1)
  # mrp_out <- mrp(
  #   data = to_adj,
  #   census = ref_table,
  #   draws = 50,
  #   chains = 2,
  #   iter = 200,
  #   cores = 1
  # )
  # # MPR
  # mrp_out <- data.frame(sample = df_name,
  #                   census = census_name,
  #                   method = 'MRP',
  #                   mrp_out
  # )
  
  # # NN
  # nn_out <- nn_poststrat(
  #   data = to_adj,
  #   census = ref_table,
  #   B = 2, # 1000
  #   n_epochs = 50, 
  #   n_neurons = 2, #8,
  #   n_hidden = 2, 
  #   batch_size = 32,
  #   learning_rate = 1e-3,
  #   early_stopping = TRUE
  # )
  # nn_out <- data.frame(sample = df_name,
  #                   census = census_name,
  #                   method = 'NN',
  #                   nn_out
  # )
  
  # append all results for this sample-census combination
  rbind(
    unweighted_out,
    poststrat_out,
    multinomial_out,
    ipw_out,
    xgboost_out,
    drp_out
    # mrp_out,
    # nn_out
  )
}

# run all iterations using lapply
out_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(iter_grid)), function(i) {
  # run one sample-census combination
  run_one(
    df_name = iter_grid$df_name[i],
    census_name = iter_grid$census_name[i],
    iter_id = i,
    n_iter = nrow(iter_grid)
  )
})

# append the results into a common dataframe
out <- do.call(rbind, out_list)

# clean row names
rownames(out) <- NULL
```
